description_long: This is a large, historic brick barn with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in weathered tiles. The structure features tall, narrow arched windows along its long sides, and a prominent triangular facade with decorative brickwork and vertical window arrangements. Attached to the main building are two smaller outbuildings with conical and hipped roofs, likely used for storage or utility purposes. The barn is surrounded by a low stone or brick wall and is set within a grassy area, with some trees and a paved path visible nearby. The overall appearance suggests a robust, traditional construction typical of European agricultural buildings, possibly repurposed for community or urban use.
description: A large, historic brick barn with a steep gable roof, tall arched windows, and attached outbuildings, set within a grassy, walled area.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long brick wall with tall arched windows and two attached outbuildings with conical and hipped roofs.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the triangular gable facade with decorative brickwork, vertical window arrangement, and adjacent outbuilding.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the long brick wall with arched windows, a smaller outbuilding, and a grassy area.
description_view_3: Front gable view with a central entrance, symmetrical window arrangement, and a clear view of the attached outbuilding to the right.
attribute_siding_pattern: brick masonry with vertical pilasters and decorative brickwork
attribute_door_style: single large hinged door at the front gable, with possible smaller utility doors on the sides
attribute_window_arrangement: tall, narrow arched windows evenly spaced along the long sides; vertical row of windows on the gable end
